meta.purpose: PPT arrow asset pack 03 gradient area arrows for page-level structure arrows, platform bands, process lanes, convergence, split, bridge, stair-step, vertical, and diagonal relations.

sources.summary: Pick for three parallel block lanes showing synchronized tracks, workstreams, or data channels. Skip for four lanes (use pptpack3_gradient_block_lanes_4_arrows).
Pick for four parallel block lanes showing multi-track execution or layered streams. Skip for three lanes (use pptpack3_gradient_block_lanes_3_arrows).
Pick for a three-stage block sequence ending in a strong arrow head. Skip for four-stage sequences (use pptpack3_gradient_block_sequence_4_plus_head).
Pick for a four-stage block sequence ending in a strong arrow head. Skip for compact chevron steps (use pptpack3_gradient_chevron_process_4_segments).
Pick for a bridge-like main chain spanning a central peak or platform relationship. Skip for low roof emphasis (use pptpack3_gradient_low_profile_roof_mainchain).
Pick for bidirectional exchange around a center node or platform. Skip for plain double-ended bars (use pptpack3_gradient_double_ended_arrow).
Pick for a central peak with two-way outward or inward horizontal direction. Skip for one-sided central lift (use pptpack3_gradient_center_peak_right_arrow).
Pick for a central platform peak sitting on a long foundation bar. Skip for bridge-only relationships (use pptpack3_gradient_bridge_mainchain_arrow).
Pick for a central lift that continues into a rightward main arrow. Skip for symmetric two-way emphasis (use pptpack3_gradient_center_peak_double_arrow).
Pick for a clean three-segment chevron process with large stage surfaces. Skip for four segments (use pptpack3_gradient_chevron_process_4_segments).
Pick for a clean four-segment chevron process with large stage surfaces. Skip for five segments (use pptpack3_gradient_chevron_process_5_segments).
Pick for a five-segment chevron process when many stages need equal visual weight. Skip for compact block sequences (use pptpack3_gradient_block_sequence_4_plus_head).
Pick for downward-right trend, transfer, or risk descent. Skip for upward trend (use pptpack3_gradient_diagonal_up_right_arrow).
Pick for upward-right growth, upgrade, or capability climb. Skip for downward movement (use pptpack3_gradient_diagonal_down_right_arrow).
Pick for a large two-ended relationship, exchange, or mutual influence bar. Skip for center-node exchange (use pptpack3_gradient_center_node_bidirectional_flow).
Pick for a ribbon arrow with notched ends on both sides, useful as a centered relation band. Skip for single-side notch ribbons (use pptpack3_gradient_left_notched_ribbon_arrow).
Pick for broad inputs narrowing into a rightward process or outcome. Skip for hourglass transformation (use pptpack3_gradient_hourglass_process_arrow).
Pick for a tunnel-like horizon path conveying passage through stages or depth. Skip for flat long arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for transform-through-a-bottleneck flows or before-after compression. Skip for one-way funnel convergence (use pptpack3_gradient_funnel_process_arrow).
Pick for a rightward ribbon with a left notch, suitable for staged headers or action bands. Skip for right notch variants (use pptpack3_gradient_right_notched_ribbon_arrow).
Pick for a dominant leftward return, rollback, or reverse-flow arrow. Skip for rightward main direction (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for a dominant rightward strategy path, migration, or action push. Skip for short pointers (use pptpack3_gradient_short_right_arrow).
Pick for a low-profile roof-like main chain that should sit behind cards or architecture layers. Skip for stronger bridge shapes (use pptpack3_gradient_bridge_mainchain_arrow).
Pick for two sides merging into a central platform or decision point. Skip for center split-out flows (use pptpack3_gradient_split_from_center_arrow).
Pick for two side inputs converging into a rightward output. Skip for center-only convergence (use pptpack3_gradient_merge_to_center_arrow).
Pick for a platform delivering downward capability, landing, or implementation. Skip for upward support (use pptpack3_gradient_platform_up_arrow).
Pick for a platform lifting upward capability, support, or enablement. Skip for downward delivery (use pptpack3_gradient_platform_down_arrow).
Pick for a ribbon relation band with a right-side notch. Skip for left notch variants (use pptpack3_gradient_left_notched_ribbon_arrow).
Pick for a compact rightward block pointer where space is limited. Skip for full-width paths (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for a central source splitting to both sides or multiple outcomes. Skip for inward convergence (use pptpack3_gradient_merge_to_center_arrow).
Pick for stepwise downward progression, rollout, or cascading delivery. Skip for upward stair-step growth (use pptpack3_gradient_stair_step_up_arrow).
Pick for stepwise upward growth, maturity, or capability climb. Skip for downward cascading delivery (use pptpack3_gradient_stair_step_down_arrow).
Pick for a broad downward movement or top-to-bottom transfer. Skip for upward motion (use pptpack3_gradient_wide_up_arrow).
Pick for a broad upward lift or foundation-to-top capability support. Skip for downward motion (use pptpack3_gradient_wide_down_arrow).
Pick for curved connector routes over, under, or around content blocks; tags: connector, route, arc, over, right. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for curved connector routes over, under, or around content blocks; tags: connector, route, arc, under, right. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for thin connector routes between nodes or lanes; tags: connector, route, cross, route, right. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for curved connector routes over, under, or around content blocks; tags: connector, route, curve, right, down. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for curved connector routes over, under, or around content blocks; tags: connector, route, curve, right, up. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).

Pick for dashed or orthogonal connector routes between structured nodes; tags: connector, route, dashed, right. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for thin connector routes between nodes or lanes; tags: connector, route, double, line, horizontal. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for thin connector routes between nodes or lanes; tags: connector, route, double, line, vertical. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for dashed or orthogonal connector routes between structured nodes; tags: connector, route, elbow, right, down. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).
Pick for dashed or orthogonal connector routes between structured nodes; tags: connector, route, elbow, right, up. Skip for large gradient area arrows (use pptpack3_gradient_long_right_arrow).

deferred.reason: Duplicate lower-impact flat variants of the imported gradient area arrows; keep outside runtime library until a recolorable flat-shape workflow is needed.
Duplicate outline variants of the imported gradient area arrows; useful as raw source but lower priority than polished PPT-style gradients.
